basic_details.job_title: NFSU Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 25 Teaching and Non-Teaching Posts
basic_details.short_information: National Forensic Sciences University (NFSU) Teaching and Non-Teaching Positions Notification 2026 has been released on 26 June 2026 on the official website nfsu.ac.in. A total of 25 vacancies have been announced through the NFSU Teaching and Non-Teaching Positions Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: Ph.D., Master's degree, Bachelor's degree in relevant discipline
qualification.details: Associate Professor (Clinical Psychology): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. 06 Years after Ph.D. or 09 years total out of which 03 Years should be after Ph.D. Registration as Clinical Psychologist under RCI Act is mandatory. Assistant Professor (Criminology): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. Master Degree in Forensic Psychology/Psychology/Behavioral Science/Criminology or relevant discipline. Assistant Professor (Finance & Accounting): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. Master Degree in Commerce/Accounting/Management/Economics/Data Science/Data Analytics/Business Intelligence or relevant discipline. Assistant Professor (General Chemistry): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. M.Sc. in Forensic Science with specialization in the appropriate branch OR first class Master Degree in Forensic Chemistry/Applied Chemistry or relevant discipline. Associate Professor (Criminal Law/Procedure Law): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. 06 Years after Ph.D. or 09 years total out of which 03 Years should be after Ph.D. Assistant Professor (Corporate Law): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. Master degree in law from a recognized University. Assistant Professor (Cyber Security): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. M.Sc./MCA/ME/M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering/Information Security/Cloud Computing/Big Data/Cyber Security/Digital Forensics or relevant discipline. Assistant Professor (Digital Forensics): Ph.D. in relevant/allied discipline with first class in preceding degree. M.Sc./MCA/ME/M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering/Information Security/Cloud Computing/Big Data/Cyber Security/Digital Forensics or relevant discipline. Junior Scientific Officer (Cyber Security): Master's Degree in Cyber Security/Digital Forensics/Information Technology/Computer Science/Electronics and communication with 02 years experience OR BE/B.Tech with 05 years experience. Scientific Assistant (Cyber Security): Master's Degree in Cyber Security Management/Digital Forensics/Information Technology/Computer Science/Forensic Science with specialization in Cyber Forensics or BE/B.Tech in relevant discipline. Lab Assistant (Cyber Security): Bachelor's degree in Cyber Security/Digital Forensics/Computer Science/Information Technology/Electronics and Communication or equivalent. Junior Scientific Officer (FPQD): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in FPQD/Forensic Science/Questioned Documents & Forensic Accounting or relevant disciplines. Scientific Assistant (Chemistry): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Chemistry/Forensic Science/Chemistry/Analytical Chemistry/Biochemistry/Forensic Pharmacology. Scientific Assistant (Toxicology): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Chemistry/Forensic Science/Chemistry/Analytical Chemistry/Biochemistry/Forensic Pharmacology. Scientific Assistant (Physics): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in Physics/Forensic Science/Forensic Physics/Advances in Physical Techniques/Audio recognition & Video analysis. Scientific Assistant (Ballistics): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics/Forensic Science/Forensic Physics/Advances in Physical Techniques/Advances in Forensic Ballistic & Armour Materials. Scientific Assistant (Biology): Master's degree in Forensic Science with specialization in Biological Sciences/Forensic Science/Forensic Biology and Anthropology/Forensic Serology and DNA profiling/Forensic Medicine. Scientific Assistant (DNA): Master's degree in Forensic Science/Forensic Biotechnology/Biotechnology/Microbiology or equivalent. Lab Assistant (Forensic): Bachelor's degree in Forensic Science or equivalent with good academic record. Scientific Assistant (Forensic Psychology): M.A./M.Sc./M.Phil in Clinical Psychology/Forensic Psychology/Criminology with specialization in Clinical Psychology. Lab Assistant (Clinical Psychology): Bachelor's degree in Clinical Psychology or equivalent with good academic record.

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the NFSU Posts Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 05-07-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of NFSU to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

rows.national_forensic_sciences_university_nf: Ph.D., Master's degree, Bachelor's degree in relevant discipline

content_sections.heading: NFSU Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the NFSU Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download NFSU Job 2026 Notification PDF

rows.event: Interview Date - School of Forensic Science & School of Management Studies
Interview Date - School of Cyber Security & Digital Forensics, School of Behavioral Forensics, School of Law, Forensic Justice & Policy Studies
Interview Date - School of Cyber Security & Digital Forensics (JSO/SA/LA)
Interview Date - School of Forensic Sciences (JSO/SA/LA)
Interview Date - School of Behavioral Forensics (SA)

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the NFSU Posts Recruitment 2026. Applications must be submitted online on or before 05-07-2026. To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section. Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of NFSU to complete the application. Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details. Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format. Pay the application fee online, if applicable. Review all details carefully before submitting the form. Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NFSU recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the NFSU Various Teaching and Non-Teaching Positions is 05-07-2026. Ensure you apply well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in this NFSU recruitment?
faq.answer: There are a total of 25 vacancies announced for various teaching and non-teaching positions at the National Forensic Sciences University.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for NFSU recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for this recruitment is strictly online. Candidates need to visit the official website to fill out and submit their applications.

faq.question: Q4. What are the minimum educational qualifications required?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualifications range from a Bachelor's degree to a Ph.D. in relevant disciplines, depending on the specific post applied for.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for SC/ST candidates?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for candidates belonging to the SC/ST category. However, General/OBC candidates need to pay INR 500 + GST/-.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary structure for the selected candidates?
faq.answer: The salary varies based on the post, ranging from Rs. 34,200/- per month for Lab Assistants to Rs. 1,94,000/- per month for Associate Professors.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these positions?
faq.answer: The selection process typically involves a screening test or skill test, followed by an interview. Specific details may vary by post.

faq.question: Q8. Can candidates from outside Delhi apply?
faq.answer: The notification mentions Delhi as the city and state. While the university is based in Delhi, candidates from other regions can apply if they meet the eligibility criteria and are willing to relocate.

faq.question: Q9. What is the job type for these positions?
faq.answer: The positions advertised are on a contractual basis.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The direct link to download the official notification PDF is provided in the "Important Links" section of this article.
